<!-- Generated by the s5-s6-implementation-plan workflow (run wf_602dec90-899): 4 research areas, adversarially critiqued; critique fixes merged into this final plan. -->

# S5 + S6 Implementation Plan — Full Builder HTTP Route Surface & Concurrency Hardening

This plan composes directly with the S1–S4 code already in place. The two load-bearing seams are unchanged:
- the **live cell** (`LiveCell` in `db/engine.swap.ts:32-37`) reassigned by `swapFromIR`;
- the **`applyEditFn` adapter closure** (`http/uws.adapter.ts:881-891`) — the full critical section (`applySchemaEdit` → `loadTypes` → `swapFromIR`).

S5 replaces the single `POST /builder` route (`uws.adapter.ts:894-912`) with the full route surface (including `POST /builder/reload`, the operator escape hatch from design §1/§3.6), folds the PUT/DELETE/preview paths onto a **single internal apply core**, and fixes the id-keying in `compose/builder.ts`. S6 wraps that core with a mutex, adds the **on-disk-derived** content-hash version, the `_builder_idempotency` table (persisted atomically with migrate), and lowers the migrate `lock_timeout`. `POST /builder` is **superseded** by `PUT /builder/content-types/:apiId` and removed (its in-process callers and tests migrate to `srv.applyEdit`, which is unchanged).

---

## S5 — Full Builder route surface

### S5.0 — `compose/builder.ts`: stable-ID-keyed `next` + drop/preview variants + pre-flight + id-ownership guard + single apply core

This is the core fix. Today (`builder.ts:70`) `next` is keyed by `apiId`, which (a) cannot drop and (b) duplicates a stable id on apiId-rename → `diff`/`indexTypes` throws. The fix is to key by stable `id`, add a delete variant and a preview variant, and route ALL of them through one apply core so `swapFromIR` is invoked from exactly one place (genuinely superseding `POST /builder`, not trading it for two closures).

#### Single apply core (collapses the PUT and DELETE entrypoints — addresses the "two closures" critique)

Factor one internal function that takes a fully-computed plan and is the ONLY caller of `migrate()` + the ONLY thing the adapter wraps for the swap:

```ts
// NEW — the single apply core. Computes nothing about WHAT to change; it is handed the resolved next/applied.
// Both PUT (applySchemaEdit) and DELETE (applySchemaDelete) build their `next` then delegate here.
async function applyResolvedPlan(
  sql: Sql,
  entitiesDir: string,
  plan: {
    applied: ContentTypeSchema[];
    next: ContentTypeSchema[];
    write?: { apiId: string; source: string };   // PUT: temp-write then rename
    removeDir?: string;                            // DELETE: rm the source dir AFTER commit
    schema?: ContentTypeSchema;                    // PUT: the addressed schema (with ids); DELETE: undefined
  },
  opts: { allowDestructive?: boolean },
): Promise<SchemaEditResult>   // { ok, blocked?, applied?, next?, schema? } — unchanged shape
```

`applyResolvedPlan` owns: the temp-file write/rename (PUT) or dir removal (DELETE), the bounded migrate retry loop (S6.d), and returns the `SchemaEditResult`. The adapter's swap closure calls `applySchemaEdit`/`applySchemaDelete` (thin builders) which delegate here; `swapFromIR` runs once in the adapter on the returned `next`. There is exactly ONE apply core feeding one swap site.

#### New/changed signatures in `compose/builder.ts`

```ts
// CHANGED — mints + pre-flight + delegates to applyResolvedPlan.
export async function applySchemaEdit(
  sql: Sql, entitiesDir: string, draft: ContentTypeDraft,
  opts: { allowDestructive?: boolean } = {},
): Promise<SchemaEditResult>

// NEW — drop a whole type (always destructive). Resolves stable id from applied; cascades/blocks inbound relations (S5.0.d).
export async function applySchemaDelete(
  sql: Sql, entitiesDir: string, apiId: string,
): Promise<SchemaEditResult>

// NEW — dry run: mint + pre-flight + migrateLint + codegen, NO file write, NO migrate apply.
export async function previewSchemaEdit(
  sql: Sql, draft: ContentTypeDraft, opts: { allowDestructive?: boolean } = {},
): Promise<{ ok: boolean; blocked: readonly Change[]; changes: readonly Change[]; schema: ContentTypeSchema; generatedSource: string }>

// CHANGED — id-ownership-aware mint. Throws BuilderValidationError → 422. REPLACES mintMissingIds entirely (not called in addition).
function resolveSchema(draft: ContentTypeDraft, appliedEntry: ContentTypeSchema | undefined): ContentTypeSchema

// NEW — pre-flight identifier/reserved/target/enum/inbound-relation validation. Throws BuilderValidationError.
function preflightValidate(schema: ContentTypeSchema, next: ContentTypeSchema[], components: { has(apiId: string): boolean }): void

// NEW typed error → mapped to 422 by the route.
export class BuilderValidationError extends Error { /* .reason: string */ }
```

`SchemaEditResult` (`builder.ts:33-43`) gains nothing at this layer (the `version`/`live` fields are added by the route envelope). Keep `{ ok, blocked?, applied?, schema?, next? }`.

#### Stable-ID-keyed `next` (replaces `builder.ts:70`)

```ts
const appliedEntry = applied.find((s) => s.id === draft.id) // upsert by client id (if any)
  ?? applied.find((s) => s.apiId === draft.apiId);          // else by apiId (an edit that omitted id)
const schema = resolveSchema(draft, appliedEntry);          // mints + ownership guard (see S5.0.c)
const next = [...applied.filter((s) => s.id !== schema.id), schema];
```

- **Create**: `schema.id` is freshly minted, not in `applied` → appended → `diff` emits `addType`.
- **Update** (same apiId): `appliedEntry` found by apiId; `resolveSchema` reuses its id → filter removes the prior entry by id, append → field diffs.
- **apiId-RENAME**: client sends `{ id: <existing>, apiId: <new> }`; filter by id removes the old entry, append the single id-stable entry with the new apiId → `diff` emits `renameType` (or throws `SchemaDiffError` if it owns relations → map 422). **Exactly one entry per id** — the duplicate-id bug is gone.
- **DELETE**: `applySchemaDelete` resolves the target stable id from the applied entry whose `apiId === apiId` (404 if none), runs the inbound-relation guard/cascade (S5.0.d), builds `next` (target removed + any cascaded relation strips), delegates to `applyResolvedPlan` with `removeDir`. `diff` emits `dropType` (+ any `dropRelation`). `swapFromIR` already dispatches `dropType` (`engine.swap.ts:78`). The source dir is removed AFTER the migrate commits (mirror the temp-file discipline).

#### (a) Pre-flight validation `preflightValidate` — runs **before** the temp-file write (`builder.ts:85`) and before `migrate()`

Ordered, each mapped to **422** by the route. Reuse the existing primitives (all THROW; wrap them):

| Check | Helper | File |
|---|---|---|
| apiId identifier + reserved + `ct_`/`_`-leading + 63-byte assembly | `deriveTableName(schema.apiId)` | `ddl.ts:232` |
| every `field.name` | `validateFieldName(f.name)` | `ddl.ts:219` |
| field-name uniqueness (case-insensitive) within draft | replicate `resolveFields` `seen` set | `content-type.repository.ts:157-164` |
| every `relation.field` + `relation.inverseField` | `validateFieldName` each | `ddl.ts:219` |
| `relation.kind` | `validateRelationKind` | `ddl.ts:181` |
| self-ref two-way `field !== inverseField` | replicate `validateRelationSpec` | `content-type.repository.ts:453` |
| relation.field collides with a scalar field / another relation field in the draft | NEW: union field-name set + relation-field set, reject dup | — |
| **enum control-char reject** | NEW: for every `enumeration` field, reject any `options.values[]` matching `/[\u0000-\u001f]/` | guards codegen `JSON.stringify` (`codegen.ts:117`) + runtime CHECK (`ddl.ts:347,461`) |
| **relation.target existence → 422** | membership in `next` (or self, lower-cased compare) | reject before `Registry.fromSchemas` throws at swap (`engine.swap.ts:47`) |
| **component / dynamiczone ref existence → 422** | collect `params.component` + `params.components[]` (mirror `referencedComponents`, `content-type.repository.ts:190`), check via `live.registry.hasComponent(apiId)` (`registry.ts:675`) | — |

The route passes `live.registry` (for `hasComponent`) and `next` into `preflightValidate`. All violations → **422**.

#### (b) The migrate-error status mappings (route layer, replaces the `400`-collapse at `uws.adapter.ts:906-907`)

The ordinary destructive-without-ack case is a **RETURN** `{ ok:false, blocked }` from `migrateLint` inside `applySchemaEdit` (`builder.ts:73-74`), NOT a throw. The others are **THROWS** from `migrate()` propagated unchanged. Map:

- return `{ ok:false, blocked }` → **409**, envelope `blocked: r.blocked`, `error: 'requires allowDestructive'`.
- `MigrationBlockedError` (thrown) → **409** (`.blocked`). (`migrate.ts:55`)
- `MigrationDataLossError` → **422** `{ table, column, affected }`. (`migrate.ts:82`)
- `MigrationUnsupportedError` → **422** (D&P/i18n toggle). (`migrate.ts:68`)
- `SchemaDiffError` (relation-owning apiId rename, `diff.ts:211`) + `BuilderValidationError` + `ddl.ts` identifier errors → **422**.
- `SchemaChangeConflictError` / leaked final `55P03` → **409** (S6.d).
- any other throw → **500**, NO `e.message` leak (tighten the current leak at `uws.adapter.ts:907`).

#### (c) `resolveSchema` — the id-ownership guard (REPLACES `mintMissingIds`, `builder.ts:46-55` — not supplemented)

Keyed strictly on the addressed type's applied snapshot entry (`appliedEntry`, matched by stable type id). Runs **before any `mintId()` write**. Renames are legal (a field keeps its id, changes its name) — the guard MUST NOT require name-equality. Throw `BuilderValidationError` → 422 on:

- **(a)** every client-supplied `field.id` MUST exist in `appliedEntry.fields[].id`; every client-supplied `relation.id` in `appliedEntry.relations[].id`. This is the one rule that catches cross-type id theft AND a NEW field carrying a non-existent/foreign id. (No name cross-check — a present id is treated as the same field, allowing rename.)
- **(b)** ids unique within the draft (no two fields/relations share an id).
- **(c)** absent id → mint via `mintId('f')`/`mintId('r')`; the minted id MUST NOT collide with any existing id.
- **(d)** `draft.id`, if present, MUST equal `appliedEntry.id` (you cannot address an arbitrary id).

`allowDestructive` is HTTP-only — strip it from the body before `resolveSchema`/codegen so it never lands in `schema.ts`.

#### (d) DELETE inbound-relation safety (BLOCKER fix — a deleted type that is the TARGET of a surviving type's relation)

`diff(applied, next)` matches every OTHER surviving type by id and sees no change to them, so it emits **no `dropRelation`** for an inbound relation a surviving type B owns pointing at deleted type A. B's link table FKs `ct_a` (`migrate.ts:254` `compileCreateLinkTable(link, ct_b, ct_a, …)`), and `compileDropTable` (`ddl.ts:491`) is a plain `DROP TABLE` with **no CASCADE** → the `dropType DROP TABLE ct_a` throws a Postgres dependency error (`2BP01`) at migrate time. Even if it succeeded, B would keep a dangling relation whose target is gone → `Registry.fromSchemas(next)` (`engine.swap.ts:47`) may throw or B serves orphaned edges.

**Decision (default): BLOCK with a clear 422.** In `applySchemaDelete`, before building `next`, scan `applied` for any **surviving** type whose `relations[].target === apiId` (case-insensitive). If any exist → throw `BuilderValidationError` → **422** with `error: 'type is referenced by <owner>.<field>; remove the relation(s) first'` listing each `<owner>.<field>`. This keeps DELETE single-purpose and the operator removes the inbound relation via a PUT first.

> Cascade (auto-emitting `dropRelation` for each inbound link + stripping the relation from each referencing type in `next` so `diff` produces a consistent catalog) is deliberately NOT the default — it silently mutates other types. It is recorded under Risks & mitigations as a future opt-in (`?cascade=true`).

A relation the **deleted type itself owns** is already handled: `diff` orders `dropRelation` before `dropType` (`diff.ts:188-191`).

---

### S5.1 — `http/uws.adapter.ts`: the route set

Register all routes inside the existing `if (entitiesDir !== undefined)` block (`uws.adapter.ts:878`), replacing `POST /builder` (894-912). Reuse `gate(res, req, 'builder.manage', true, …)` (`:476`), `parseBody` (`:677`), `corkSend`, and capture `:apiId` via `req.getParameter(0)` and any request header via `req.getHeader(...)` **synchronously** before any await (as `ctMutate` does at `:849`). The legacy `/content-types/*` 410 (`:848`) stays.

The **uniform envelope** helper (NEW, module-scope):

```ts
function builderEnvelope(status: number, fields: {
  ok: boolean; version: string; schema?: ContentTypeSchema; schemas?: ContentTypeSchema[];
  applied?: readonly Change[]; blocked?: readonly Change[]; error?: string; currentVersion?: string;
  table?: string; column?: string; affected?: number;
}): CoreResponse  // body always carries applied:[]/blocked:[]/live:true; conditional fields set only when present
```

`applied` and `blocked` are **always present** (empty arrays on no-op) so the SPA never branches on status to learn "nothing changed".

Routes:

1. **`GET /builder/content-types`** — PUBLIC (no gate, like `handleContentTypeRoute` GET at `:866`). `readAppliedSchemas(sql)` (`migrate.ts:281`) for the body → `200 { ok:true, schemas, version }`. `version` is the cached on-disk version (S6.b). Sets `ETag: version`; honors `If-None-Match` (read via `req.getHeader` up-front) → `304` on match.

2. **`GET /builder/content-types/:apiId`** — PUBLIC. Find applied entry by apiId; 404 if absent; else `200 { ok:true, schema, version }` (the single `ContentTypeSchema` WITH ids — the client needs them to echo for ownership + If-Match). `ETag: version`; `If-None-Match` → `304`.

3. **`POST /builder/content-types/:apiId/preview`** — GATED. `parseBody`; `body.apiId === apiId` else 422; `previewSchemaEdit(sql, body, { allowDestructive })` (mint + `preflightValidate` + `migrateLint` + `generateSchemaSource`, **no write/migrate**). `200 { ok, blocked, applied: changes, schema, generatedSource, version }` where `version` is the CURRENT (unchanged) catalog version. Assert-side: no `_schema_applied` mutation, no `ct_<apiId>` table.

4. **`PUT /builder/content-types/:apiId`** — GATED. Flow (corrected order — version precheck is INSIDE the mutex, see S6.a/S6.b):
   1. `parseBody` → `body.apiId === apiId` else **422**.
   2. read `If-Match` / `Idempotency-Key` headers synchronously.
   3. require `If-Match` or `body.version` present, else **428** Precondition Required (committed; no hedge).
   4. opportunistic idempotency TTL prune (OUTSIDE the mutex, S6.c).
   5. **acquire mutex** (`writerBusy` try-acquire, else **409 + `Retry-After`**).
   6. `try { … } finally { writerBusy=false }`:
      - idempotency lookup (replay path — no migrate/swap; S6.c).
      - recompute the **live on-disk** version (S6.b); compare to `If-Match`/`body.version`; mismatch → **412 `{ currentVersion }`**.
      - `applyEditFn(body, { allowDestructive: body.allowDestructive === true })` → map per S5.0.b.
      - on success: post-flip on-disk version recompute (set `currentVersion`); the idempotency row is persisted atomically with migrate (S6.c).
   7. `200` envelope (with the new `version`) on success; skip version bump on no-op.

5. **`DELETE /builder/content-types/:apiId`** — GATED. Body `{ allowDestructive?, version? }` + `If-Match`/`Idempotency-Key` headers. Drop is ALWAYS destructive → require `allowDestructive === true`, else **409** (`error: 'requires allowDestructive'`). Require `If-Match`/`body.version` else **428**. Same mutex/version/idempotency wrap (identical order to PUT), then `applySchemaDelete(sql, dir, apiId)` (forces `allowDestructive` internally; runs S5.0.d inbound guard → 422; 404 if the type doesn't exist). On success, swap (the `dropType` change drives `swapFromIR`).

6. **`POST /builder/reload`** — GATED (HIGH fix — the operator escape hatch from design §1/§3.6; load-bearing for the version contract). Cache-busted re-import of on-disk files + rebuild + version recompute, NO migrate:
   - acquire the same mutex (a reload mutates `live` and must not interleave with an apply).
   - cache-busted `loadTypes(entitiesDir)` — append `?v=<counter/now>` to import specifiers so an out-of-band `schema.ts` edit is actually re-read (ESM module cache otherwise serves the stale copy).
   - rebuild via the existing reload primitive (`swapFromIR` / `loadFromSchemas` on the freshly imported IR) — no DDL, no `migrate()`.
   - **recompute the on-disk version INTO this primitive** (after the cache-busted import), set `currentVersion`. This is what makes a pre-reload PUT carrying the old version fail 412.
   - `200 { ok:true, version }`.

   The single apply core uses the SAME cache-busting `loadTypes` path so applies and reloads agree on what "on disk" means.

`applyEditFn` (`:881-891`) is unchanged internally; it is now invoked through the S6 mutex and feeds the single apply core. For DELETE, the closure still runs `swapFromIR` — `applySchemaDelete` returns the same `SchemaEditResult` shape (`ok, applied:[dropType…], next, schema:undefined`), and the swap-guard (`!result.ok || next===undefined || applied.length===0`) passes because `applied` is non-empty.

### S5 env-gate seam

Compute a `builderEnabled` boolean once (default `process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'`; overridable by an explicit flag the caller threads — design the seam, do not implement the prod-toggle policy here, that is S11). When `builderActive && !builderEnabled`, the four mutating routes (`preview`/`PUT`/`DELETE`/`reload`) return **404** (route absent, indistinguishable from no-builder); the GET routes may stay (read-only). The seam lives at the top of the `if (entitiesDir !== undefined)` block.

---

## S6 — Concurrency hardening

### S6.a — App-level single-writer async mutex

The critical section is **not** inside `applySchemaEdit` — it ends at `swapFromIR` in the adapter closure (`uws.adapter.ts:889`). So the mutex lives in the adapter and wraps the apply core + the version precheck + idempotency.

- Module-scope (per-server) `let writerBusy = false;`. Single-instance, single-threaded JS — no `await` between check and set, so a boolean try-acquire is race-free.
- In each mutating handler, acquire the mutex **FIRST** (before ANY catalog/version read), then do version-precheck → idempotency → apply ALL inside the held mutex (corrected vs the draft, which read the precheck outside — that reopened the lost-update race two concurrent PUTs both passing 412 then serializing):
  ```ts
  if (writerBusy) return corkSend(res, aborted, { status: 409, contentType: JSON_CT,
    body: Buffer.from(JSON.stringify({ ok:false, error:'builder busy' })), headers: { 'Retry-After': '1' } });
  writerBusy = true;
  try {
    // recompute live on-disk version → If-Match compare (412)
    // idempotency lookup (replay) / proceed
    // applyEditFn|applyDeleteFn → swapFromIR
    // post-flip version recompute + idempotency persist
  } finally { writerBusy = false; }
  ```
  Do **not** await/queue — `tryAcquire` and reject with **409 + `Retry-After`**.
- Expose the same guard around `UwsServer.applyEdit` (`:1481`) so programmatic callers serialize.
- The migrate advisory lock (`pg_advisory_xact_lock(MIGRATE_LOCK_KEY)`, `migrate.ts:376`) STAYS as defense-in-depth. Holding the mutex across the version read → apply core → `swapFromIR` makes the documented double/triple catalog read TOCTOU-safe within the instance.
- `CoreResponse` must carry custom headers — see S6.e.

### S6.b — Optimistic concurrency: content-hash version derived from ON-DISK files (BLOCKER fix)

The version MUST track the source of truth — the **files**, not `_schema_applied`. A snapshot-derived token would NOT advance when an operator edits `schema.ts` on disk and runs `POST /builder/reload` (reload never migrates), so a later PUT carrying the old version would pass the 412 check while the on-disk source has diverged. This matches the committed design §4 and is NOT re-opened.

- `computeCatalogVersion(entitiesDir): Promise<string>` (NEW, in `compose/catalog-version.ts`):
  - cache-busted `loadTypes(entitiesDir)` (append `?v=` so an out-of-band edit is re-read; reuse the same helper the reload primitive uses).
  - canonicalize the resulting IR: stable key order + `ORDER BY id` on the type list and on each `fields`/`relations` array.
  - `createHash('sha256').update(JSON.stringify(canonicalIR)).digest('hex')`. Import `createHash` from `node:crypto`.
  - `request_hash` for idempotency (S6.c) canonicalizes the request body the SAME way.
- **Cache** `let currentVersion: string` on the server next to the live cell / mutex.
- **Recompute at the three points the design names:**
  - **GET** (routes 1+2): return the cached `version` + `ETag`. Honest because apply/reload always recompute.
  - **On apply** (inside the mutex, AFTER `swapFromIR` returns successfully): recompute from the now-on-disk catalog (the temp file has been renamed into place), set `currentVersion`, return it in the 200 envelope. **Skip on no-op** (swap skipped → version unchanged).
  - **On reload/boot**: wire the recompute INTO the reload primitive (after the cache-busted re-import), and once after `reconcileBoot` (`conti.ts:91`). Set `currentVersion` at server construction (compute after-listen or lazily on first GET).
- **If-Match enforcement (PUT/DELETE):** compute the precheck version **inside the mutex window** (one cache-busted `computeCatalogVersion`), compare against the client `If-Match` (or `body.version`). Mismatch → **412 `{ currentVersion }`**. Neither header nor `body.version` present → **428** (committed). Match → proceed.

### S6.c — Persisted idempotency: `_builder_idempotency` (written atomically with migrate — BLOCKER-adjacent fix)

`ensureIdempotencyTable(sql)` (NEW, mirror `ensureAppliedTable`, `migrate.ts:271`):

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _builder_idempotency (
  key text PRIMARY KEY,
  request_hash text NOT NULL,   -- sha256(method + ':' + apiId + ':' + canonical(body)) — same canonicalization as the version
  status int NOT NULL,
  response jsonb NOT NULL,       -- the full stored envelope { ok, version, schema?, applied, blocked, live }
  created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
)
```

Flow on a PUT/DELETE carrying `Idempotency-Key`:
1. `ensureIdempotencyTable(sql)`; `SELECT status, response, request_hash FROM _builder_idempotency WHERE key = $1` (under the mutex).
2. HIT + matching `request_hash` → **replay** stored `status` + `response` verbatim. No migrate, no swap. (DELETE replays a `schema`-less envelope — `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` means `schema` was never set; the replay reproduces it exactly.)
3. HIT + different `request_hash` → **422** (key reused for a different request).
4. MISS → proceed. **Persist the idempotency row INSIDE the same migrate transaction that runs `reconcileApplied`**, so the row is atomic with the durable DB+catalog commit. (The draft inserted AFTER `swapFromIR`, which loses the record on the swap-phase-3 / connection-drop window — `loadAllRelations` runs after `live` is mutated and the DB/file are committed, `engine.swap.ts:84-86`, and a throw there rethrows → 500 with the migrate already committed; a retry with the same key would then re-run the whole apply and re-mint.) Writing it inside the migrate tx means a swap-phase-3 throw replays a 200 whose engine state self-heals on next boot — the accepted single-instance contract.
   - Thread the key + precomputed envelope + `request_hash` into `migrate()`/`applyResolvedPlan` so the INSERT rides the same tx as `reconcileApplied`. Use `INSERT … ON CONFLICT (key) DO NOTHING`.
5. **Store terminal 4xx outcomes too** (e.g. 422 data-loss, 409 requires-destructive), keyed, so a Stripe-style retry of a deterministically-failing request replays the same failure. **Never store transient** `409 builder busy` or lock-timeout `409` (those are meant to be retried fresh). Terminal-4xx rows are written in their own short tx (no migrate ran).
6. **Re-mint guard (no key):** independent of the key — `resolveSchema`'s id-ownership guard (S5.0.c) already rejects an id-less retry against an existing type (a known field supplied without its id is treated as a NEW mint, but rule (a) requires client ids for fields that exist in the snapshot → **422**). This catches a client that retries WITHOUT a key.
- **TTL:** opportunistic prune — `DELETE FROM _builder_idempotency WHERE created_at < now() - interval '24 hours'`, run **OUTSIDE the mutex** (before acquire) and only when an `Idempotency-Key` is present, so it never adds latency under the single-writer lock. No background job (single-instance).
- Add `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS _builder_idempotency` to the test `beforeEach` teardown.

### S6.d — `lock_timeout` lowering + bounded retry on contention

- Lower `SET LOCAL lock_timeout = '5s'` → **literal `'1500ms'`** at `migrate.ts:374` (the ONLY lock_timeout the migrate path sets; `applyChangeSet`). Leave `runSchemaTx`'s 5s (`ddl.ts:609`) alone — distinct path.
- Bounded retry around the `migrate()` call **inside the apply core** (`applyResolvedPlan`), structured so the temp file written for PUT survives across attempts and is unlinked **once** on final failure:
  ```ts
  let result; let lastErr;
  for (let attempt = 0; attempt < 3; attempt++) {
    try { result = await migrate(sql, next, opts); lastErr = undefined; break; }
    catch (err) {
      if ((err as { code?: string }).code === '55P03') { lastErr = err; await sleep(jitter(attempt)); continue; }
      await unlink(tmp).catch(() => {}); throw err;  // non-lock error: unlink + rethrow as today
    }
  }
  if (lastErr) { await unlink(tmp).catch(() => {}); throw new SchemaChangeConflictError('builder migrate lock contended; retry'); }
  ```
  Retry on **`55P03` only** (lock_timeout). `40P01` (deadlock) / `40001` (serialization) are dropped from the retry set — the single-writer mutex + advisory lock serialize all schema writes within the instance, so those codes are not reachable here and would be untested branches (medium fix). `migrate()` re-reads applied + re-diffs each attempt (correct and cheap). Backoff: small jitter (e.g. `50 * 2**attempt + random`).
- **Explicit max wall-clock bound:** 3 attempts × `lock_timeout` 1500ms + 2 backoffs (≈50 + 100ms + jitter) ≈ **~4.7s worst case**, well under the test/HTTP timeout. Asserted in test #18.
- The adapter maps `SchemaChangeConflictError` (and a leaked final `55P03`) → **409** (mirror `ddl.ts:621` → `content-type.controller.ts:180`). `SchemaChangeConflictError` is already exported from `ddl.ts`; import it in `builder.ts`.

### S6.e — `CoreResponse` custom headers (Retry-After / ETag / version)

`CoreResponse` is `{ status, contentType, body }` (`read.router.ts:48`); `writeResponse` (`uws.adapter.ts:97`) writes only status + Content-Type + body, and is on the shared read hot path.

- Add optional `headers?: Record<string,string>` to `CoreResponse`. In `writeResponse`, iterate `headers` AFTER Content-Type; when absent (the normal read path) the output is **byte-identical** to today.
- Thread `headers` through `corkSend`.
- For the public GETs, read `If-None-Match` via `req.getHeader` synchronously up-front (before any await), like `getParameter`.
- Add an explicit test that a normal read response (no headers set) is unchanged.

---

## How this composes with S1–S4 (explicit)

- **Live cell + `swapFromIR`** (`engine.swap.ts`): UNCHANGED. It already dispatches `dropType`/`renameType` (`:59,78`). S5 only makes `next` express them.
- **`applyEditFn` closure** (`uws.adapter.ts:881-891`): UNCHANGED internally; now invoked through the S6 mutex and feeds the single apply core (`applyResolvedPlan`); a thin DELETE builder reuses the same core + swap site (no second swap closure).
- **`POST /builder`** (`:894-912`): REMOVED, superseded by `PUT /builder/content-types/:apiId`. In-process tests/callers use `srv.applyEdit` (unchanged signature).
- **`applySchemaEdit`** (`builder.ts`): the `next` line changes id→key; pre-flight + ownership guard added before temp-write; both edit and delete delegate to `applyResolvedPlan` (retry loop + idempotency-in-tx live there).
- **Boot** (`conti.ts:91`): add `currentVersion = await computeCatalogVersion(entitiesDir)` after `reconcileBoot`.

## Invariants respected

`erasableSyntaxOnly` (typed errors are classes, no enums/param-properties — match `MigrationBlockedError` shape); `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` (envelope conditional fields and `applyEdit` key set only when present); single-instance (boolean mutex, opportunistic TTL, no Redis); byte-identical reads (swap path untouched; version is on-disk metadata only; the read hot path is unchanged when no headers are set); files-as-source-of-truth (`schema.ts` written/removed atomically; version + idempotency derive from / commit with the files and `reconcileApplied` tx; `_schema_applied`/`_builder_idempotency` on-demand created, no hand-written migration).

---

## Risks & mitigations (folded medium/low)

- **DELETE cascade vs block (medium, deferred).** Default is BLOCK-with-422 on inbound relations (S5.0.d). A future opt-in `DELETE …?cascade=true` could auto-emit `dropRelation` for each inbound link + strip the relation from each referencing type in `next`. Not built now — cascade silently mutating other types is a sharp edge; an operator removing the inbound relation via PUT first is explicit and safe.
- **`lock_timeout` retry wall-clock budget (medium).** Bounded at ~4.7s worst case (3×1500ms + backoff); asserted in test #18 so it cannot silently grow. Only `55P03` is retried; `40P01`/`40001` intentionally excluded (unreachable under single-writer mutex + advisory lock) to avoid untested branches.
- **`writeResponse` header plumbing on the hot path (medium).** `headers?` is optional and only iterated when set; the no-header read path is byte-identical and guarded by an explicit unchanged-read test (S6.e).
- **id-ownership guard subtlety (medium).** The guard keys strictly on the addressed type's snapshot (`appliedEntry`), runs before any mint, and does NOT require name-equality (renames are legal). The sole forge it blocks is a client id that is not in THIS type's snapshot (cross-type theft / foreign id). `mintMissingIds` is removed, not supplemented.
- **TTL prune cost (low).** `DELETE … created_at < now()-24h` has no index on `created_at` (PK is `key`) → seq scan, but runs OUTSIDE the mutex, only on keyed requests, on a tiny rare-admin table → negligible single-instance. Add an index only if the table is observed to grow.
- **428-vs-accept (low).** Committed: **428 Precondition Required** when neither `If-Match` nor `body.version` is present on PUT/DELETE. No accept-and-skip mode; test #15 is unconditional.
- **Idempotency for failures (low/medium).** Terminal 4xx outcomes are stored and replayed; transient 409-busy / lock-timeout are never stored (retried fresh).

---

## TEST PLAN (real PG via `createFileDatabase`, all assertions over `fetch`, NO mocks)

**Harness (hybrid `before()` — neither existing helper does both):** build the server with `createServer(engine, store, registry, undefined, auth, sessionCache, rbac, undefined, new HookRegistry(hooks), genDir)` so `builderActive && authEnabled` are both true. Lift `signUp`/`grantRole`/`userIdOf` from `auth.route-gating.e2e.test.ts:38,60,53` into `test/helpers.ts`. First `signUp` (with `origin: base` header for better-auth CSRF) = super-admin (holds `builder.manage`). Add `put`/`del`/`preview`/`reload` fetch helpers with `If-Match`/`Idempotency-Key`/`If-None-Match`/`cookie` header variants, alongside `get`/`post`/`dataOf` (`engine-swap.e2e.test.ts:45-48`). `beforeEach`: `cleanCatalog(sql)` + `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS _schema_applied` + `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS _builder_idempotency` + `rm(genDir, {recursive,force})` + reopen server. Use `tableExists`/`physicalColumns` (`helpers.ts:44,50`) for DB-side assertions. NEVER re-`loadTypes` to assert; assert only via HTTP GET.

### S5

1. **PUT add/modify live** — `PUT /builder/content-types/gadget` `{apiId:'gadget',fields:[...]}` + cookie + `If-Match` from prior GET → 200 uniform envelope; then `GET /gadget` 200, `POST /gadget` 201, `GET /builder/content-types/gadget` reflects the field; `tableExists(sql,'ct_gadget')` true.
2. **DELETE drops a type** (proves id-keyed next) — seed via PUT, GET to capture version, `DELETE /builder/content-types/gadget` `If-Match: V` → 200; `GET /gadget` 404; `tableExists(sql,'ct_gadget')` false; `_schema_applied` row gone.
3. **apiId RENAME** (proves id-keyed next) — seed `gadget`, GET to capture stable `id` + version; `PUT /builder/content-types/gadget` `{id:<same>,apiId:'doohickey',...}` → 200; `GET /doohickey` 200, `GET /gadget` 404; data carried.
4. **422 bad/reserved identifier** — PUT each: `fields:[{name:'1bad'}]`, `name:'id'`, `name:'created_at'`, `name:'_x'`, a relation `field:'document_id'`, an `inverseField:'locale'` → each 422.
5. **422 enum control-char** — PUT an `enumeration` field whose `options.values` contains `"a\nb"` / `"\u0000"` → 422; assert no file written, `_schema_applied` unchanged.
6. **422 dangling relation target** — PUT a relation `{field:'author',target:'nope'}` → 422.
7. **422 missing component ref** — PUT a `component` field referencing an unregistered component → 422.
8. **422 id-ownership** — (a) PUT `gadget` with `field.id` belonging to a different type's snapshot → 422; (b) client type `id` ≠ gadget's snapshot id → 422; (c) NEW field carrying a non-existent id → 422; (d) duplicate ids within the draft → 422; (e) **legit rename** (same field id, new name) → **200** (guard must NOT block renames).
9. **422 body/path mismatch** — `PUT /builder/content-types/gadget` with `body.apiId:'other'` → 422.
10. **preview makes NO write** — `POST /builder/content-types/gadget/preview` → 200 with `applied[]`/`blocked[]`/`generatedSource`; then `tableExists(sql,'ct_gadget')` UNCHANGED, `GET /gadget` 404, `SELECT count(*) FROM _schema_applied` unchanged.
11. **migrate error mapping** — (a) destructive shrink-on-overlong-rows with `allowDestructive` → `MigrationDataLossError` → **422** `{table,column,affected}`; (b) blocked destructive WITHOUT `allowDestructive` → **409** + `blocked[]`; (c) a `setTypeOption` toggle → `MigrationUnsupportedError` → **422**; (d) apiId-rename of a relation-owning type → `SchemaDiffError` → **422**.
12. **auth** — PUT/DELETE/preview/reload with no cookie → 401; with a cookie lacking `builder.manage` → 403; GET routes → 200 with no cookie (public).
13. **DELETE of a relation-TARGET type → 422** (BLOCKER fix) — seed type A and type B where B owns a relation `target: 'a'`; `DELETE /builder/content-types/a` with `allowDestructive + If-Match` → **422** `error` naming `b.<field>`; `tableExists(sql,'ct_a')` still true, `ct_a` link table intact, `GET /a` still 200. Then `PUT b` removing the relation, then `DELETE a` → 200.

### S6

14. **Single-writer mutex** — drive two `srv.applyEdit(draft)` promises in-process with edit A's migrate measurably slow (large fixture) so B hits the held mutex; OR `Promise.allSettled([put(A), put(B)])` — assert exactly ONE 200 and the other **409 + `Retry-After`** header.
15. **Stale version → 412** — GET → V1; successful PUT → V2; second `PUT … If-Match: V1` → **412** + `body.currentVersion === V2`.
16. **Missing If-Match → 428** (unconditional) — PUT and DELETE with no `If-Match` and no `body.version` → **428**.
17. **Idempotency replay** — `PUT … Idempotency-Key: K` → 200, capture envelope; identical second PUT same `K` → byte-identical stored envelope, schema/field ids identical, `_schema_applied` written exactly once (row count / `applied_at` unchanged). Different-body same `K` → 422. **Terminal-4xx replay:** a deterministically-failing PUT (e.g. enum control-char → 422) with `K2`, retried with `K2` → same **422** replayed. **Transient not stored:** a 409-busy is NOT persisted (a later same-key request is processed fresh).
18. **Re-mint guard** — id-less retry (`fields` without ids) against an existing `gadget` WITHOUT a key → 422 (not a duplicate-mint).
19. **lock_timeout → 409 bounded** — open a SECOND independent `postgres(db.url)` handle, `BEGIN; SELECT pg_advisory_xact_lock(0x5c3ea901)` and HOLD; fire a PUT; assert it returns **409** within the asserted **~4.7s** bound (measure wall-clock, assert `< ~5s`); release the held lock in `finally`.
20. **Version recompute on reload (on-disk source — BLOCKER fix)** — apply an edit (V→V2), `GET` → V2. Then write an out-of-band change to `schema.ts` on disk (a round-trippable presentation change that leaves `_schema_applied` byte-identical), `POST /builder/reload` → 200 with a NEW version V3 ≠ V2 (proves the version tracks files, not the snapshot); `GET` → V3; a `PUT … If-Match: V2` after the reload → **412** (the stale-version-passes guard the on-disk hash exists to enforce).
21. **GET ETag / 304** — `GET /builder/content-types` → `ETag: V`; repeat with `If-None-Match: V` → **304**; after a PUT, `If-None-Match: V` (old) → **200** with new version.
22. **read hot path unchanged** — a normal content read (`GET /gadget`) returns a response with no extra headers (byte-identical to pre-S6 `writeResponse` output) — guards the `headers?` addition (S6.e).

**Files:** edit `packages/api/src/compose/builder.ts`, `packages/api/src/http/uws.adapter.ts`, `packages/api/src/http/read.router.ts` (`CoreResponse.headers?`), `packages/api/src/db/schema/migrate.ts` (lock_timeout + `ensureIdempotencyTable` + idempotency-INSERT-in-tx), `packages/api/src/compose/conti.ts` (version on boot); add `packages/api/src/compose/catalog-version.ts`; new tests `packages/api/test/builder-route.e2e.test.ts` (S5) + `packages/api/test/builder-concurrency.e2e.test.ts` (S6); lift `signUp`/`grantRole`/`userIdOf` into `packages/api/test/helpers.ts`.

---

## Ordered, checkable step list

### S5 (do first)

- [ ] **S5-1** Add `CoreResponse.headers?: Record<string,string>` (`read.router.ts`) and iterate it in `writeResponse` after Content-Type (no-op when absent); thread `headers` through `corkSend`. (Prereq for Retry-After/ETag.)
- [ ] **S5-2** In `compose/builder.ts`: add `BuilderValidationError`; write `resolveSchema` (id-ownership guard, S5.0.c) and DELETE `mintMissingIds`.
- [ ] **S5-3** Write `preflightValidate` (S5.0.a) reusing `deriveTableName`/`validateFieldName`/`validateRelationKind` + the new enum-control-char + relation-target + component-ref checks.
- [ ] **S5-4** Factor `applyResolvedPlan` (single apply core: temp-write/rename or dir-remove, `migrate()` call site, returns `SchemaEditResult`).
- [ ] **S5-5** Rewrite `applySchemaEdit` to: mint via `resolveSchema`, build id-keyed `next`, `preflightValidate`, delegate to `applyResolvedPlan`.
- [ ] **S5-6** Write `applySchemaDelete`: resolve target id, run the inbound-relation BLOCK guard (S5.0.d → 422), build `next` (target removed), delegate to `applyResolvedPlan` with `removeDir`.
- [ ] **S5-7** Write `previewSchemaEdit` (mint + preflight + migrateLint + codegen, no write/migrate).
- [ ] **S5-8** In `uws.adapter.ts`: add `builderEnvelope`; capture `:apiId` + headers synchronously; remove `POST /builder` (894-912).
- [ ] **S5-9** Add routes `GET /builder/content-types`, `GET /builder/content-types/:apiId` (public, ETag/304), `POST …/:apiId/preview` (gated), `PUT …/:apiId` (gated), `DELETE …/:apiId` (gated), `POST /builder/reload` (gated, cache-busted import + rebuild, no migrate). Wire the S5.0.b error→status mapping.
- [ ] **S5-10** Add the `builderEnabled` env-gate seam (mutating routes → 404 when disabled).
- [ ] **S5-11** Migrate in-process callers/tests off `POST /builder` to `srv.applyEdit`.
- [ ] **S5-12** Write `builder-route.e2e.test.ts` (tests 1–13); make them green.

### S6 (do after S5)

- [ ] **S6-1** Add `compose/catalog-version.ts`: `computeCatalogVersion(entitiesDir)` via cache-busted `loadTypes` → canonical IR → sha256. Define the shared cache-busting `loadTypes` helper used by both apply core and reload.
- [ ] **S6-2** Add `currentVersion` cache on the server; set it at construction, after `reconcileBoot` (`conti.ts:91`), inside the reload primitive (post cache-busted import), and post-flip on every successful apply (skip no-op).
- [ ] **S6-3** Add the boolean `writerBusy` mutex; acquire FIRST in every mutating handler; release in `finally`; 409 + `Retry-After` on contention. Wrap `UwsServer.applyEdit` with the same guard.
- [ ] **S6-4** Move the If-Match version precheck INSIDE the mutex (recompute live on-disk version → compare → 412 `{currentVersion}`); enforce 428 when neither `If-Match` nor `body.version` is present.
- [ ] **S6-5** Add `ensureIdempotencyTable` (`migrate.ts`); thread key + envelope + `request_hash` so the INSERT rides the `reconcileApplied` tx; replay on matching hash, 422 on hash mismatch; store terminal 4xx (own tx), never store transient 409; opportunistic TTL prune OUTSIDE the mutex.
- [ ] **S6-6** Lower `lock_timeout` to `'1500ms'` (`migrate.ts:374`); add the `55P03`-only bounded retry (3 attempts, jitter backoff, single final unlink) in `applyResolvedPlan`; map `SchemaChangeConflictError`/leaked `55P03` → 409.
- [ ] **S6-7** Add `If-None-Match`/`ETag` 304 handling to the public GET routes.
- [ ] **S6-8** Write `builder-concurrency.e2e.test.ts` (tests 14–22), including the on-disk reload-version test (#20) and the unchanged-read-hot-path test (#22); make them green.
